results.possibleIdentity: Philip Pollack was an electrical engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s who was employed by the Sperry Gyroscope Company. Based in Wantagh, New York, he specialized in technical fields including radar systems and electronic circuits. Pollack was an active participant in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, a non-profit that facilitated collaborations between artists and engineers. Within E.A.T., he provided technical expertise for artistic projects, most notably collaborating with the Austrian sound artist Bernhard Leitner. Pollack assisted Leitner in the development of specialized electronic circuits for sound installations such as the 'Sound Cube' (Ton-Würfel), which utilized multi-channel audio to create a sculptural experience of sound. His professional address during this period was recorded as 1421 Wantagh Avenue in Wantagh, NY.
